page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an Intel processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 128 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Single-core performance is moderate, landing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core tests. The hardware excels in specific workloads, achieving top-tier (top 10%) results in image processing, static web serving throughput, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large block read and write operations fall into the average tier.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is designed for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The inclusion of AMX instructions makes it highly capable for CPU-based machine learning tasks, as demonstrated by strong performance in LLM prompt processing for small and medium models. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, software compilation, and parallel processing tasks. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient choice for pure compute scaling where external storage is utilized.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 128 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. Powered by an Intel processor with a 260 MB L3 cache, it supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, focusing resources entirely on compute density. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core performance, with top-tier results in image processing, static web serving, and database operations. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support.
tagline: A high-density compute server featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM optimized for compute-heavy tasks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix math
Top-tier performance in static web serving and database operations
Strong multi-core benchmark results for parallel processing and compilation
No local storage or GPU resources, maximizing CPU-to-cost efficiency
